Step 1:
The Snap Suggestion

-1-
Pattern Interrupt
Optional personal introduction and optional image
Take an element from your Nightmare Story for image
If no image is used, use a linguistic interrupt; Use power
words/phrases and write using the Headline Formula

-- 2 of 54 --

Pattern Interrupt Image Example
Hi, I’m Jon…And This Is
A Fish…

-- 3 of 54 --

Pattern Interrupt Image Example (With Subliminal Highlighting Technique)
And In A Few Minutes, You’ll Find
Out Why This Fish Is The 	Secret
To Your Weight Loss…

-- 4 of 54 --

Hi, I’m Jon…In Just A Few
Minutes, You’ll 	Discover 	The
“Magic Pill Answer” To Your
Weight Loss Goals…
Pattern Interrupt Linguistic Interrupt Example (With Subliminal Highlighting Technique)
NOTE: My “Shocking Statement” is the phrase “Magic Pill Answer”

-- 5 of 54 --

-2-
The Big Promise
Part 1: The Suggestion
Encourage the viewer to watch the video until the end
Part 2: 	The Outrageous Benefit
This is taken from your USP Benefits. Unless you choose to
use an avoid approach, you should structure these benefits
like this: “You’ll discover (tip) without (1-3 “without”
statements.) If you use an avoid tip, do not use the “withouts”

-- 6 of 54 --

Watch 	This Short Video Until
The End To Discover The
3 	Fastest 	Ways To Lose That
Unwanted Weight…
The Big Promise Example: Using Without Statements

-- 7 of 54 --

Without 	Starvation…And
Definitely Without Ever Having
To Sacrifice Eating Your
Favorite Foods…
The Big Promise Example: Using Without Statements

-- 8 of 54 --

Watch 	This Short Video Until
The End To Discover The 3
Supposedly “Healthy” Foods You
Absolutely 	Must Avoid 	To Lose
That Unwanted Weight…
The Big Promise Example: Using an avoid tip example

-- 9 of 54 --

-3-
Video Scarcity
This is where you warn the viewer that the free video they
are watching will not be online forever. If you can give a
specific reason for the scarcity, do so.

-- 10 of 54 --

Just A Bit Of 	Fair Warning:
I Do Not Know How Long This
Video Will Be Online…
Video Scarcity Example

-- 11 of 54 --

I’m Giving Away So Much 	Free
And 	Hard-To-Find 	Weight Loss
Information That I May End Up
Making This A Paid
Presentation…
Video Scarcity Example

-- 12 of 54 --

So 	Watch This Now, While
It’s Still Online…And Still Free…
Video Scarcity Example

-- 13 of 54 --

-4-
Something Completely
Different
Let the viewer know that your video contains “secrets”,
“unique ideas”, “little-known facts”, etc…and that it is
like nothing they have seen before.

-- 14 of 54 --

Now, You May Think You’ve Seen
And Heard It All When It Comes
To Weight Loss…
Something Completely Different Example

-- 15 of 54 --

Yet I Can 	Guarantee 	You That
You’ve Never Seen These 	Little-
Known 	Weight Loss Tips
Before…
Something Completely Different Example

-- 16 of 54 --

This Is Entirely Different:
It’s Nothing Like The Popular
Diet Books You May Have
Seen…Or Even Tried…
Something Completely Different Example

-- 17 of 54 --

So Prepare To Be 	Shocked
By What I 	Reveal 	To You Today…
Something Completely Different Example

-- 18 of 54 --

-5-
The “Let’s Make A Deal”
Transition
This transitions your viewer into your Nightmare Story and
Step 2 of the 5-Step VSL Process: The Vital Connection.
The goal here is to (a) reinforce the promise of the benefits
the user will discover; yet (b) open a loop by not going into
those benefits and tips, letting the viewer know that you will
be sharing a personal story first. You conclude by saying
( c ) “Is that fair?” or simply, “Deal?”

-- 19 of 54 --

And Remember:
What I’m Sharing Will Finally
Allow You The Freedom Of
Losing Weight Without Cramping
Your Lifestyle…
Let’s Make A Deal Example

-- 20 of 54 --

And Give You Some Of My Best
Weight Loss 	Secrets 	To Make
Sure You Continue To Drop All
The Weight You Desire…
Let’s Make A Deal Example

-- 21 of 54 --

Quickly, Painlessly, And In A
Unique Way That’s Actually
Fun 	To Do!...
Let’s Make A Deal Example

-- 22 of 54 --

First, I Need To Tell You
A 	Story…
Let’s Make A Deal Example

-- 23 of 54 --

It’s A Story That I’m Pretty
Embarrassed To Share…
Let’s Make A Deal Example

-- 24 of 54 --

Yet It’s One You 	Absolutely
Need 	To Hear Because It Will
Reveal More About How You Too
Can Lose Weight Than Anything
I Know… 	Deal?
Let’s Make A Deal Example

The 5-Step VSL Process™
Step 2:
The Vital Connection
© 2011 And Beyond | Jon Benson / 3X Formulas, Inc.

-- 26 of 54 --

-1-
The Modest Introduction
This is where you covertly sneak in some of your credentials
and expertise on the topics you are covering, without making
the viewer feel alienated or drop out of rapport.

-- 27 of 54 --

Now, I Was Not Always A
Fitness And Weight Loss
Expert…
Modest Introduction Example

-- 28 of 54 --

Appearing On Magazine
Covers…Helping People All
Over The World Lose Weight…
Modest Introduction Example

-- 29 of 54 --

And Certainly Not In 	Top Shape
Like I Am Today…
Modest Introduction Example

-- 30 of 54 --

In Fact:
Far, Far From It…
Modest Introduction Example

-- 31 of 54 --

Actually, I Was 	Obese…
(Optional image here and additional slide to prove the point)
Modest Introduction Example

-- 32 of 54 --

Actually, I Was 	Obese…
Modest Introduction Example With Image (Image Is Not Me; This Is Just For Demonstration Purposes)

-- 33 of 54 --

I’ll Show You My “After” 	Pics
Later On… First, Let Me Share A
Rather Shocking Story With You:
Modest Introduction Example With Image

-- 34 of 54 --

-2-
Your Nightmare Story
Take your Nightmare Story and, using one sentence at a time
followed by an ellipses (…), place it on the next series of
slides. Do not concern yourself with length. As long as you’ve
followed the Nightmare Story Formula, your viewer will fall
into deeper rapport during this section.

-- 35 of 54 --

-2-
Your Nightmare Story Goes Here
And For As Many Slides As It
Requires

-- 36 of 54 --

-3-
Transition Into The Big Problem
You now prepare your viewer for Step 3 of The 5-Step
VSL Solution: The Big Problem. Here, you let the viewer
know that (a) you are just like them, however: (b) You just
discovered a System/Formula/Blueprint/Secret/etc. You
conclude with Inclusion Statements and positive benefits.

-- 37 of 54 --

So, As You Can 	Clearly See,
I’m Not Some Super-Stud
Genetic Wonder…
Transition Into The Big Problem
